Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given an event log of a Customer Support Ticket Resolution process with 5 cases IDs 101 to 105 Each case has events with timestamps We need to identify cases with significantly longer total resolution times determine potential root causes of delays and propose recommendations

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets calculate total resolution time for each case from Receive Ticket to Close Ticket

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 101 Receive 2024 03 01 08 00 Close 2024 03 01 10 15 Duration 2 hours 15 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 102 Receive 2024 03 01 08 05 Close 2024 03 02 09 15 Duration 1 day 1 hour 10 minutes Actually from 08 05 to next day 09 15 25 hours 10 minutes Lets compute from 08 05 to 08 05 next day is 24 hours plus 1 hour 10 min 25 hours 10 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 103 Receive 2024 03 01 08 10 Close 2024 03 01 09 30 Duration 1 hour 20 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Receive 2024 03 01 08 20 Close 2024 03 02 08 30 Duration 24 hours 10 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 105 Receive 2024 03 01 08 25 Close 2024 03 03 09 30 Duration 2 days 1 hour 5 minutes From 08 25 to 08 25 next day 24h to 08 25 day after 48h then to 09 30 48h plus 1h5m 49 hours 5 minutes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: So clearly cases 102 104 105 are significantly longer than 101 and 103 103 is the shortest 101 is short 105 is extreme

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now identify patterns escalations Case 101 no escalation Case 102 escalates to L2 agent Case 103 no escalation Case 104 no escalation but time from Investigate to Resolve is long Case 105 escalates to L2 after Investigate So both 102 and 105 have escalations 104 does not have escalation but still long

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets examine time gaps between activities for each case Possible causes waiting times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 101 timeline Receive 08 00 Triage 08 10 plus 10 min Assign L1 08 20 plus 10 min Investigate 09 00 plus 40 min Resolve 10 00 plus 1 hr Close 10 15 plus 15 min Total cycle 2h15m Appears smooth no long waits

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Receive 08 05 Triage 08 30 plus 25 min Assign L1 09 00 plus 30 min Escalate L2 11 30 plus 2h30m Investigate 14 00 plus 2h30m Resolve 09 00 next day from 14 00 to next day 09 00 is 19 hours Close 09 15 plus 15 min Long wait between Escalate L2 and Investigate is 2h30m then Investigate to Resolve is overnight 19h Also between Assign L1 and Escalate is 2h30m So many long idle times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 103 Receive 08 10 Triage 08 15 plus 5 min Assign L1 08 30 plus 15 min Investigate 08 45 plus 15 min Resolve 09 15 plus 30 min Close 09 30 plus 15 min Very short quick progression

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Receive 08 20 Triage 09 00 plus 40 min Assign L1 09 30 plus 30 min Investigate 13 00 plus 3h30m Resolve 08 00 next day plus 19 hours Close 08 30 plus 30 min No escalation but long wait before investigation 3h30m and very long wait from investigation to resolve overnight

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Receive 08 25 Triage 08 35 plus 10 min Assign L1 09 00 plus 25 min Investigate 09 10 plus 10 min Escalate L2 10 00 plus 50 min Investigate on 2024 03 02 14 00 From Escalate at 10 00 on day 1 to Investigate at 14 00 next day thats 28h Then Resolve 03 03 09 00 plus 19h Close 09 30 plus 30 min Massive delays especially between Escalate and the next Investigate and then between that Investigate and Resolve

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Potential root causes Escalations Cases 102 and 105 have escalations leading to additional handoffs and wait times In 102 after escalation theres a 2h30m wait before L2 investigation starts then another long wait overnight before resolution In 105 after escalation theres a 28 hour wait before the next investigation maybe waiting for L2 availability then another long wait overnight before resolve

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In Case 104 no escalation but still long wait before investigation 3h30m might be due to L1 agent backlog or unavailability Then investigation at 13 00 but resolve next day 08 00 an overnight gap suggesting that the investigation might have started late in the day and resolution required next business day or agent went off shift

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Overall long waiting times between activities suggest resource unavailability or shift patterns The long gaps overnight indicate that work is not 24 7 tickets wait overnight

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In 104 investigate starts at 13 00 and resolve next day 08 00 In 102 escalation happened at 11 30 then L2 investigation at 14 00 then resolve next day 09 00 In 105 L2 investigation started at 14 00 next day then resolution next day 09 00

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: To identify patterns we can compare the durations of each phase for all cases

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Phase 1 Receive to Triage 101 10 min 102 25 min 103 5 min 104 40 min 105 10 min Average about 18 min 104 is a bit higher 40 min but not extreme

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Phase 2 Triage to Assign L1 101 10 min 102 30 min 103 15 min 104 30 min 105 25 min Average about 22 min all within similar range

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Phase 3 Assign L1 to Investigate for non escalated or Assign L1 to Escalate to Investigate For cases without escalation 101 40 min 103 15 min 104 3h30m For cases with escalation 102 total 5h from Assign to L2 Investigate 105 huge delay after escalation of 28h

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Phase 4 Investigate to Resolve 101 1h 102 19h 103 30 min 104 19h 105 after L2 Investigate 19h So 19h overnight gap appears in all long cases 102 104 105 Shorter cases 101 and 103 are resolved within the same working day

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So it seems if investigation starts early enough in the day resolution happens same day If it starts in the afternoon it stretches overnight

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: For 104 assigned at 09 30 but investigation did not start until 13 00 That could be due to L1 agent being busy with other tickets or ticket sitting in queue

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: For 102 L2 started at 14 00 but resolution did not happen same day maybe the issue required more than a few hours or L2 agents shift ended before finishing

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: For 105 L2 investigation started at 14 00 next day after 28h wait That indicates L2 backlog

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Another factor In case 105 there is a duplicate Investigate Issue activity one by L1 before escalation then another by L2 after escalation This might indicate rework or L2 starting over adding cycle time

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Summary Long cases 102 25h10m 104 24h10m 105 49h5m Short cases 101 2h15m 103 1h20m

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Factors causing delays Escalations to L2 with significant waiting time for L2 availability in cases 102 and 105 In 105 L2 was unavailable for over a day Late start of investigation in case 104 Overnight gaps investigations that start in the afternoon often spill over to next business day extending resolution time by about 19h Potential workload imbalances or limited L2 capacity causing backlog Inefficient handoffs between L1 and L2 in 105 with possible redoing of work

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations Analyze L1 and L2 agent capacity and workload If L1s are overburdened causing delays in starting investigations consider staffing adjustments If L2 has long queues hire more L2 agents or improve prioritization

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Implement shift scheduling or after hours support for high priority tickets to avoid overnight delays especially if faster resolution is required

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Streamline escalation process perhaps allow L1 to directly involve L2 without a full separate investigation or provide better knowledge base so escalations are reduced

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Monitor the Assigned to Investigation time as a key performance indicator set targets such as start investigation within 1 hour of assignment and alert when exceeded

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Use ticket prioritization not all tickets require the same service level but the long ones might indicate systemic issues
